channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why Uber and Lyft drivers are using risky DIY Tesla robotaxis
item.description: A self-driving Tesla carrying a passenger for Uber rammed into an SUV at an intersection in suburban Las Vegas in April, an accident that sparked new concerns that a growing stable of self-styled “robotaxis” is exploiting a regulatory gray area in U.S. cities, putting lives at risk.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems
item.description: While there’s no such thing as a silver bullet solution to secure the public sector, continuous pentesting addresses these issues head-on. The post Addressing the public sector’s penetration testing problems first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI continues to burn money
item.description: Although OpenAI’s revenues are increasing significantly, the generative AI (genAI) pioneer remains dependent on financial injections, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big Fremont office park occupied by German tech titan lands buyer
item.description: Prologis, a Bay Area real estate behemoth, has paid $71.3 million to buy four research and office buildings on Bayside Parkway.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: How data brokers are fueling elder fraud in America
item.description: Criminals are increasingly turning to data brokers to provide access to private information that can be used to improve their scamming schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk's Republican Donations: A Shift in Political Allegiance?
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk's significant donations to Republican causes, including support for Trump adviser Stephen Miller and Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, have raised eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How not to waste 34 hours a month in meetings
item.description: None of the participants will ever forget this meeting, that much is certain. The venue was the US Department of Defense, starring a new Secretary of State responsible for procurement, who was to lead her first meeting with suppliers and colleagues from her own company.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Uber and Lyft Fund Opposition to San Francisco's Proposition L
item.description: San Francisco ballot initiative aims to increase funding for public transit system by taxing ride-hailing companies, but Uber and Lyft are pushing back with opposition efforts
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI Raises $6.6 Billion in Venture Capital, Shifts Away from Nonprofit Roots
item.description: OpenAI raises record-breaking $6.6 billion, valuing the company at $157 billion as it moves towards a for-profit model
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fix for BGP routing insecurity ‘plagued by software vulnerabilities’ of its own, researchers find
item.description: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) is not yet the simple fix for the security weaknesses of the internet’s Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) many in the communications industry think it is, a team of German researchers has warned.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Beware of fraudsters posing as government officials trying to steal your cash
item.description: CISA warns of scammers impersonating its officials. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges people to be alert and verify contacts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk sending Starlink terminals to storm-ravaged North Carolina
item.description: SpaceX CEO Elon Musk said Tuesday the aerospace company is sending more Starlink terminals to North Carolina following the widespread devastation of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline
item.description: The Defense Department’s zero trust framework is acting as a catalyst, driving mission owners to industry in search of zero trust solutions The post DoD agencies confront zero trust challenges, misunderstandings ahead of 2026 deadline first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft unveils new AI-powered Windows 11 features for Copilot+ PC users
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday unveiled the latest AI features for Copilot+ PCs, with improved Windows search and a tool that can detect and act on the contents of a user’s screen.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Newsom throws AI regulation fight into uncertainty with veto
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom's (D) decision to veto a sweeping artificial intelligence (AI) bill has renewed the debate over the future of regulation of the technology, leaving different sectors of the tech industry largely in disagreement on the best path forward.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Think twice before using public computers: Here's why
item.description: Using public computers poses security risks, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says, so take precautions or use your own secure device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO
item.description: Christopher Skaluba, the executive director in the DoD CDAO, said the office is creating tools to provide data-driven insights on AI workforce needs. The post DoD seeks to create ‘culture of learning’ through CDAO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
